message:
feat: implement epoch-scoped fencing, health-gate enforcement, rollout state validation, durability verification, and degraded-mode security policy

Connector layer (sections 10.15/10.16 integration):
- fencing.rs: Adds epoch-stamped fencing tokens with fail-closed
  rejection of tokens from stale configuration epochs. Fencing tokens
  now carry the control epoch they were issued under, and the validation
  path rejects tokens whose epoch falls outside the canonical validity
  window.
- health_gate.rs: Implements health-gate policy enforcement with
  epoch-scoped validity. Health gates block rollout progression when
  health checks fail, and gate decisions are invalidated when the
  control epoch advances.
- rollout_state.rs: Extended to carry epoch stamps on rollout plans,
  ensuring that rollout state from a previous epoch cannot influence
  decisions in the current epoch. Adds validation that rollout
  checkpoints are within the canonical validity window.
- durability.rs: New module implementing durability violation detection
  and bundling — detects when stored data integrity falls below the
  tier-mandated threshold and generates violation bundles for the
  repair subsystem.

Security layer:
- degraded_mode_policy.rs: Defines behavior when the node enters
  degraded mode (e.g., lost quorum, integrity sweep failures). Specifies
  which operations are permitted, which are blocked, and how the node
  signals its degraded state to peers.
- security/mod.rs: Registers the degraded-mode policy module.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
